name: Chun's Reef
tagline: A mellow North Shore reef break named after surf legend John Chun — long, cruisy longboard waves that let beginners feel the real North Shore.
parking: Roadside parking along Kamehameha Highway; no lot, just pull off the shoulder
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) for the gentlest conditions; manageable on smaller winter days too. Morning for glassy water
talkStory: Named after local surf legend John Chun who surfed here with his family through the 1960s, Chun's Reef is where North Shore locals graduate from Puaena Point's whitewater to real reef break surfing. The wave is a long, forgiving right that peels over a shallow reef — not quite beginner-beginner, but perfect for someone who's already stood up a few times and wants to try reading a real wave. Several North Shore surf schools use Chun's for their intermediate sessions. Be mindful of the reef bottom and wear reef shoes. The vibe is mellow and local — no one's showing off here, just cruising on longboards. On flat summer days it's a calm, clear spot for snorkeling too.
